findings.message: New public endpoint `count_items` (GET /items/count) has no docstring, unlike the sibling `get_items` endpoint and the /health, /heartbeat, /metrics routes which all document purpose and contract. Undocumented public interface.
findings.suggestion: Add a docstring explaining what the endpoint returns and when to use it (e.g. "Return the total number of items in the store. Convenience endpoint for clients that need a count without paginating /items.").

findings.message: Return type is annotated as a bare `dict` and no `response_model` is declared, so the response contract (shape `{"count": int}`) is undocumented in code and in the generated OpenAPI spec. The sibling route uses an explicit `ItemRead` response_model for exactly this reason.
findings.suggestion: Define a small response schema (e.g. `class ItemCount(BaseModel): count: int`) and use `@router.get("/items/count", response_model=ItemCount)` with a `-> ItemCount` return annotation so the contract is explicit and the OpenAPI spec is accurate.

findings.message: README's `## Endpoints` section enumerates `/heartbeat`, `/health`, `/metrics`, and `/items` but does not mention the newly added `/items/count` route. The README endpoint list is now stale.
findings.suggestion: Add a bullet under `## Endpoints` in README.md: `- `GET /items/count` — count of items in the store`. Also remember to run `task openapi:export` and commit the regenerated `openapi.json` (the README notes CI fails on a stale spec).

findings.message: Implementation loads every row via `list_items(session)` just to take `len(...)`. The choice (favouring simplicity over a `SELECT count(*)`) is non-obvious and undocumented; a future reader may assume it is an oversight.
findings.suggestion: Either switch to a dedicated `count_items` repository function using `SELECT count(*)` or add a one-line comment/docstring noting the deliberate simplification (and any row-count ceiling under which it is acceptable).
